reasoning: The stock price dropped significantly from the upper 90s in late March/early April to around 86.12 as of April 11, exacerbated by a Morgan Stanley downgrade from Equal-Weight to Underweight on April 7, 2025. This introduces negative short-term momentum. The first date (April 20) resolves to the April 17 close, occurring just before the April 22 earnings report. Due to the recent downgrade and negative momentum, the probability for a short-term increase above the April 11 threshold is modeled slightly below 50%. The later dates (May, July, October) occur after the upcoming Q1 earnings report, where analysts foresee a 9.4% YoY jump in non-GAAP EPS. Therefore, while near-term pressures weigh the price down, the longer horizons naturally introduce more variance and mean-reversion toward 50%, conforming to the instruction that uncertainty should increase (bringing probabilities closer to 0.5) over time.
